comment_text.sn14.15:1.1: The seven good monks here are among the Buddha’s greatest disciples, all of whom were regarded as possessing great qualities of character and spiritual prowess. They each had their own speciality, which attracted students of a similar character.
comment_text.sn14.15:2.23: Devadatta was the Buddha’s cousin and nemesis. After going forth together with several other relatives of the Buddha (Kd 17:1.4.1), his initial success in meditation was corrupted by his desire for gains and fame (SN 17.35). He tried to take over the Saṅgha but was denounced by the Buddha. His attempt to create a schismatic faction failed and he died in disgrace.

root_text.sn14.15:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.sn14.15:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā sāriputto sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati;
root_text.sn14.15:1.3: āyasmāpi kho mahāmoggallāno sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati;
root_text.sn14.15:1.4: āyasmāpi kho mahākassapo sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati;
root_text.sn14.15:1.5: āyasmāpi kho anuruddho sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati;
root_text.sn14.15:1.6: āyasmāpi kho puṇṇo mantāniputto sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati;
root_text.sn14.15:1.7: āyasmāpi kho upāli sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati;
root_text.sn14.15:1.8: āyasmāpi kho ānando sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati;
root_text.sn14.15:1.9: devadattopi kho sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ bhagavato avidūre caṅkamati.
root_text.sn14.15:2.2: “passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, sāriputtaṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:2.5: Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, moggallānaṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:2.8: Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, kassapaṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:2.11: Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, anuruddhaṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:2.14: Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, puṇṇaṁ mantāniputtaṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:2.17: Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, upāliṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:2.20: Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, ānandaṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:2.23: Passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, devadattaṁ sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ caṅkamantan”ti?
root_text.sn14.15:3.4: Atītampi kho, bhikkhave, addhānaṁ dhātusova sattā saṁsandiṁsu samiṁsu.
root_text.sn14.15:4.1: Anāgatampi kho, bhikkhave, addhānaṁ dhātusova sattā saṁsandissanti samessanti.
root_text.sn14.15:5.1: Etarahipi kho, bhikkhave, paccuppannaṁ addhānaṁ dhātusova sattā saṁsandanti samenti.

translation_text.sn14.15:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.sn14.15:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Sāriputta was walking together with several mendicants not far from the Buddha.
translation_text.sn14.15:1.3: Venerable Mahāmoggallāna was doing likewise, as were
translation_text.sn14.15:2.1: Then the Buddha said to the mendicants,
translation_text.sn14.15:2.2: “Mendicants, do you see Sāriputta walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.4: “All of those mendicants have great wisdom.
translation_text.sn14.15:2.5: Do you see Moggallāna walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.7: “All of those mendicants have great psychic power.
translation_text.sn14.15:2.8: Do you see Kassapa walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.11: Do you see Anuruddha walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.14: Do you see Puṇṇa son of Mantāṇī walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.16: “All of those mendicants are Dhamma speakers.
translation_text.sn14.15:2.17: Do you see Upāli walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.19: “All of those mendicants are experts in monastic law.
translation_text.sn14.15:2.20: Do you see Ānanda walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.22: “All of those mendicants are very learned.
translation_text.sn14.15:2.23: Do you see Devadatta walking together with several mendicants?”
translation_text.sn14.15:2.25: “All of those mendicants have corrupt wishes.
translation_text.sn14.15:3.1: Sentient beings join together and converge because of an element.
translation_text.sn14.15:3.2: Those who have inferior convictions join together and converge with those who have inferior convictions.
translation_text.sn14.15:3.3: Those who have good convictions join together and converge with those who have good convictions.
translation_text.sn14.15:5.1: and also in the present, sentient beings join together and converge because of an element.
translation_text.sn14.15:5.2: Those who have inferior convictions join together and converge with those who have inferior convictions.
translation_text.sn14.15:5.3: Those who have good convictions join together and converge with those who have good convictions.”
